goal: 官网首页只保留一个 Hub 入口

summary.title: Keep one public Hub entry on the website
summary.what: Removed the GitHub Pages Hub snapshot as a second public website CTA and removed that duplicate URL from the website README, AI knowledge data, and llms files. The website now presents /hub/ as the single public Hub entry.
summary.why: Showing both the website Hub and the GitHub Pages snapshot made users ask what the difference was; the product surface should have one obvious Hub entry.
summary.user_goal: 官网首页只保留一个 Hub 入口

decisions.chose: Use https://mainline.sh/hub/ as the only public Hub entry on the website.
decisions.rationale: The website domain is the user-facing product surface, and one CTA avoids forcing users to distinguish two equivalent static exports.

decisions.chose: Remove the secondary GitHub Pages snapshot button from the self-Hub section.
decisions.rationale: The section should send readers to Hub, not ask them to choose between two hosting paths.

decisions.chose: Remove GitHub Pages snapshot references from aiKnowledge and llms files.
decisions.rationale: Search and agent-readable surfaces should learn the same single canonical Hub entry as human readers.

rejected.alternative: Rename the second button to explain that GitHub Pages is a backup snapshot.
rejected.reason: The user explicitly agreed that there is no need to distinguish the two in the public UI.

rejected.alternative: Keep GitHub Pages as a public canonical page in llms files.
rejected.reason: That would preserve the same ambiguity in machine-readable surfaces.

review_notes: Ran npm run build: Astro check reported 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 hints; Astro built 61 pages.
Ran git diff --check and git diff --cached --check.
Confirmed no mainline-org.github.io/mainline, GitHub Pages snapshot, or pagesCta references remain in website public/source files checked.

architectural_claims: The website-owned Hub route, /hub/, is the canonical public Hub entry.
GitHub Pages may still exist as infrastructure, but it is not a separate public choice in the website UI or AI-readable website surfaces.
behavioral_changes: Homepage self-Hub section now has one Hub button.
Website README, llms files, and AI knowledge data expose only the mainline.sh Hub URL.
security_implications: No auth, runtime data access, or permission behavior changed.
migration_notes: No migration required; /hub/ remains the existing public Hub path.
